title: rubus incarnatus
intro: Rubus incarnatus, also known as the pink-flowering raspberry or salmonberry, is a perennial shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its high content of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Rubus incarnatus include its ability to support digestive health, boost the immune system, and exhibit potential anti-cancer properties. Rubus incarnatus is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and can also be consumed as a fresh fruit or dried as a dietary supplement.
